title: Your '$55K Salary' Is Actually $17 an Hour
hook: Your employer says you make fifty-five thousand a year — but once you count what your job actually costs you, your real hourly wage might make you sick.
angle: The 'true hourly wage' calculation accounts for taxes, unpaid commute hours, and work-related expenses — revealing that the typical salaried worker earns 30-40% less per hour than their stated wage suggests.
audience: Salaried employees aged 25-45 who feel like they work hard but never seem to get ahead financially
key_numbers: 225 unpaid commute hours per year for the average American
$5,000 in annual work-related expenses (clothes, lunches, gas, stress spending)
$37,900 real take-home after taxes and work costs on a $55K salary
$17.04 true hourly wage vs $27.50 stated hourly wage
